heading: Expanded broadcasting in countries of the former Soviet Union
label: 8927 Expanded broadcasting in countries of the former Soviet Union
notes: Change of NameBroadcasting Board of Governors renamed United States Agency for Global Media pursuant to . The renaming was effectuated by notice to congressional appropriations committees dated , and became effective .section 6204(a)(21) of this titleMay 24, 2018Aug. 22, 2018

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Chairman of the Broadcasting Board of Governors shall submit to Congress a plan, including a cost estimate, for immediately and substantially increasing, and maintaining through fiscal year 2017, the quantity of Russian-language broadcasting into the countries of the former Soviet Union funded by the United States in order to counter Russian Federation propaganda.December 18, 2014

children.heading: Prioritization of broadcasting into Ukraine, Georgia, and Moldova
children.label: b Prioritization of broadcasting into Ukraine, Georgia, and Moldova
children.text: The plan required by subsection (a) shall prioritize broadcasting into Ukraine, Georgia, and Moldova by the Voice of America and Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty.
near-term increases in Russian-language broadcasting for countries of the former Soviet Union (other than the countries specified in subsection (b)), including Latvia, Lithuania, and Estonia; and
increases in broadcasting in other critical languages, including Ukrainian and Romanian languages.

children.heading: Broadcasting defined
children.label: d Broadcasting defined
children.text: In this section, the term “broadcasting” means the distribution of media content via radio broadcasting, television broadcasting, and Internet-based platforms, among other platforms.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Broadcasting Board of Governors $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2016 through 2018 to carry out activities under this section.

children.heading: Supplement not supplant
children.label: 2 Supplement not supplant
children.text: Amounts authorized to be appropriated pursuant to paragraph (1) shall supplement and not supplant other amounts made available for activities described in this section.
